channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Jury Rules Against Elon Musk in Lawsuit Claiming OpenAI Strayed from Original Mission
item.description: A US federal jury in Oakland, California, has ruled against Elon Musk in his 2024 lawsuit against OpenAI, concluding that the AI company is not liable for allegedly departing from its founding mission...

item.title: US Extends Temporary Waiver on Russian Oil, Allowing India Continued Imports
item.description: The United States has extended a temporary waiver allowing Russian crude oil already in transit to be delivered, despite sanctions. This extension, valid until June 17, provides India and other buyers...

item.title: Wastewater analysis reveals Kenya's hidden drug abuse trends
item.description: In Kenya, a groundbreaking nationwide wastewater study by Nacada has unveiled a worrying picture of drug abuse across the country. Covering eight regions and 12 hotspot counties, 152 sewage samples we...

item.title: Taguig Woman Arrested for Allegedly Abusing Partner’s Child
item.description: Authorities in Taguig have arrested a woman in connection with an alleged case of child abuse involving the child of her female partner. According to reports, the woman allegedly inflicted physical ha...

item.title: Imran Khan Seeks Supreme Court Approval for Hospital Transfer and Medical Access
item.description: ISLAMABAD: Former Prime Minister Imran Khan has approached the Supreme Court of Pakistan seeking orders for his transfer to Shifa International Hospital in Islamabad for proper medical examination and...

item.title: Rediscovery of Rare Australian Plant Ptilotus senarius After Nearly Six Decades
item.description: A plant thought to be extinct for almost 60 years, Ptilotus senarius, has been rediscovered in northern Australia thanks to a chance observation by a citizen scientist. Aaron Bean, a horticulturist, p...

item.title: Kansas City Chiefs Expected to Bounce Back in 2026 with Mahomes Returning
item.description: After missing the playoffs last season for the first time in the Patrick Mahomes era due to an ACL injury, the Kansas City Chiefs are set to aim for a strong return in 2026. Mahomes is expected to ret...

item.title: ICE Officer Faces Charges After Nonfatal Shooting in Minnesota Amid Trump-Era Immigration Policies
item.description: Authorities in a Minnesota county have formally charged an Immigration and Customs Enforcement (ICE) officer following a nonfatal shooting incident that occurred during the period of former President ...

item.title: Fazlur Rehman Calls on President Zardari to Safeguard 18th Amendment Amid Political Concerns
item.description: On May 19, 2026, JUI-F chief Maulana Fazlur Rehman publicly urged President Asif Ali Zardari to ensure the protection of the 18th Amendment, emphasising its historical significance and the role the PP...

item.title: Max Scherzer resumes pitching as Blue Jays monitor recovery from forearm injury
item.description: Toronto Blue Jays ace Max Scherzer is gradually returning to action following right forearm tendinitis. On Monday at Yankee Stadium, the 41-year-old pitcher threw a bullpen session of roughly 22 pitch...

item.title: The Greek Herald: 100 Years Connecting Australia's Greek Community
item.description: Marking a century since its founding, The Greek Herald remains a cornerstone of Australia's Greek community, preserving language, culture, and social connection. Established in 1926 as The Hellenic He...

item.title: Teenagers post videos of stolen motorcycles on TikTok, frustrating victims and police
item.description: A BBC investigation has uncovered hundreds of TikTok videos showing teenagers openly boasting about stealing motorcycles and mocking their victims. In many instances, social media has even been used t...

item.title: MEA clarifies India’s media reach and citizen rights during Oslo press briefing
item.description: During a press conference in Oslo, the Ministry of External Affairs (MEA) of India responded firmly to questions from the Norwegian press regarding press freedom and governance. MEA Secretary (West), ...

item.title: Two Teenagers Attack San Diego Mosque, Resulting in Four Deaths
item.description: Authorities in San Diego are investigating a tragic shooting at a local Islamic centre that left three men dead, along with the two teenage suspects who carried out the attack. According to law enforc...

item.title: Proof-of-Concept Shows Windows MiniPlasma Zero-Day Still Exploitable
item.description: A new Windows zero-day vulnerability, dubbed 'MiniPlasma', has been demonstrated to still grant SYSTEM privileges even on fully patched Windows 11 systems. The vulnerability was originally reported by...

item.title: Financial and Legal Considerations for Short-Term Home Rentals During the World Cup
item.description: As the FIFA World Cup approaches, many Canadians are tempted to rent out their homes on platforms like Airbnb, Vrbo, or Booking.com to earn extra income. While advertising potential earnings of over $...

item.title: Jose Luis Montales appointed as Senate Secretary and Impeachment Court Clerk
item.description: Jose Luis Montales has been officially elected as the new Senate secretary by senators through a voice vote, following the appointment of Alan Peter Cayetano as the Senate President. Montales will als...

item.title: Archbishop Martins urges FG to allow citizens verify foreign loans usage
item.description: The Catholic Archbishop of Lagos, Most Rev. Alfred Adewale Martins, don urge the Federal Government make e dey serious about accountability by creating clear ways wey citizens fit check infrastructura...

item.title: Canada’s Energy Sector Is Important but Not a Global Superpower
item.description: In recent discourse, Canada has increasingly been described as an energy superpower, a notion seemingly embraced by the federal government as leverage in international trade discussions, including the...

item.title: NPA prosecutor suspended following missed court appearance in Sibanyoni extortion case
item.description: The National Prosecuting Authority (NPA) has taken disciplinary action against a prosecutor who failed to appear in the Kwaggafontein Magistrates Court in Mpumalanga on Monday, leading to the case of ...
